rationale: Target home identified as the large, complex hip-roofed structure in the center of the image at approximately x:435–650, y:375–680 px. Bounding box measured at ~215 px wide × ~305 px deep (45.6 ft × 64.6 ft), giving a raw bounding area of ~2,943 sq ft. Applied a 75% fill factor to account for the numerous hip bays, valleys, and cutouts yielding ~2,207 sq ft footprint. The roof is entirely hip-style (no gable ends, so rakes = 0). Ridge lines are short horizontal peaks atop each hip section (~5 segments). Hip lines are numerous, running diagonally from each ridge end to each corner (~18 total). Valley lines are deep inward intersections between projecting wings and the main body (~8 total). Eaves run along the lower horizontal edges of each hip section (~14 segments). Confidence is moderate-low due to image complexity, multiple overlapping structures in dense development, and difficulty in precisely delineating individual unit boundaries.
